description: Configure the field laptop's Chrome browser to support offline-ready telemetry downloading, location-aware airspace checks, and 3D flight planning. Organize industry-specific bookmarks, grant precise geolocation permissions, optimize Chrome for downloading massive orthomosaic datasets, enable WebGL flags for 3D flight path visualization, and configure a custom NOTAM search engine.
instruction: Read the flight operations standard document on your Desktop (~/Desktop/flight_ops_standard.txt). Organize the scattered bookmarks into the 5 specified folders (Airspace, Weather, Photogrammetry, Hardware, Off-Duty). Allow location permissions for Aloft.ai and UAVForecast. Configure your download directory to ~/Documents/Mission_Data/Raw_Images and TURN OFF the download prompt so batch downloads don't hang. Enable 'Parallel downloading' and 'WebGL Draft Extensions' in chrome://flags. Create a custom search engine with the keyword 'notam' pointing to 'https://notams.aim.faa.gov/notamSearch/search?notam=%s'. Set your startup pages to open Aloft and UAVForecast automatically.
